items.title: Bund collapse floods 32 homes in Banting
items.summary: BANTING: Thirty-two houses in Taman Langat Utama 2, RTB Bukit Changgang, were affected by flooding after a nearby river bund burst last night. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the incident occurred about 11pm, with most residents having already evacuated the area. However, the department only received an emergency call at 4.12am, prompting six personnel from the Banting Fire and Rescue Station to be deployed.

items.title: Fake and altered MyKad images used to secure jobs, NRD warns employers
items.summary: KLANG: Employers have been warned against relying solely on photographs of MyKad displayed on mobile phones when hiring workers, following the discovery of a tactic involving altered identity card images used to secure employment. National Registration Department (NRD) director-general Datuk Badrul Hisham Alias said the modus operandi involved replacing the photograph on another person's MyKad before saving the image on a mobile phone and presenting it during the hiring process. "The individual takes an identity card, changes the photograph and keeps the image on a phone.

items.title: New-look MyKad draws strong public response
items.summary: KLANG: Applications for the new-look MyKad have received an overwhelming response from the public since June 1, the National Registration Department (NRD) said. NRD director-general Datuk Badrul Hisham Alias said the government would first use its remaining stock of existing MyKad cards before rolling out the new version nationwide. Saifuddin had said new applications for the two documents would not immediately receive the upgraded versions, as the government plans to clear remaining stock before rolling out the new high-security documents.

items.title: Police seek public’s help to trace missing woman last seen leaving Kamunting home for job interview
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Police are seeking the public’s assistance to locate a 41-year-old woman who has been reported missing in Taiping since June 16, according to Sinar Harian . Acting Taiping police chief Supt Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said police received a report on the disappearance of Nor Afiza Zainuddin at 11.22am on June 18. Mohd Ariffridzuan said police later found the Perodua Axia at a recreational area in Kampung Paya Lintang, Batu Hampar near Padang Rengas on June 21.

items.title: Ketamine and Erimin 5 ring smashed in Perak, 17-year-old among three suspects
items.summary: IPOH, June 25 — Police arrested three individuals, including a 17-year-old, after dismantling a drug trafficking syndicate involving ketamine and Erimin 5 pills worth RM120,050 in Pengkalan Tiara here on Monday. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) of the Perak Contingent Police Headquarters (IPK) carried out the raid at 8.45 pm after receiving intelligence on drug trafficking activities in the area. Mohd Alwi said initial urine screening tests showed that one suspect tested positive for ketamine, while the other two tested negative.

items.title: Court of Appeal urges caution over repeat prosecutions
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The public prosecutor should remain vigilant against practices that may create a perception of repeated prosecutions arising from the same investigation, the Court of Appeal said yesterday. Judge Datuk Noorin Badaruddin said the integrity of the criminal justice system is strengthened when prosecutorial decisions promote fairness to the accused and finality in litigation. Noorin said this in her grounds of judgment dismissing DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng, his wife Betty Chew and businesswoman Phang Li Koon's appeal to strike out corruption and money laundering charges linked to a RM11.6 million foreign workers' hostel project.

items.title: Melaka Form Two student dies after collapsing during rugby training, says state exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — A Form Two student died after collapsing during a rugby training session at a school in Telok Mas, Melaka yesterday, according to Sinar Harian . The victim’s grand-uncle, Yahya Endut, 64, said he was shocked by the incident involving the teenager, whom he had helped raise since childhood. Meanwhile, Melaka education, higher education and religious affairs committee chairman Datuk Rahmad Mariman confirmed the incident and said the student collapsed during a post-training briefing before being pronounced dead.

items.title: [UPDATED] Puad Zarkashi quits Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Puad Zarkashi today announced his immediate resignation from the party, saying that Johor Umno was no longer free to make its own decisions. In a statement, he said his resignation, effective today, would spare Umno the need to expel him and allow him to openly express views that differed from the party's leadership without being accused of undermining it from within. "The main reason I am leaving Umno is because Johor Umno is no longer free to make its own decisions," he said.

items.title: Foreign workers caught using relatives' MyKad, fake identity cards in Klang raid
items.summary: KLANG: A 20-year-old Indonesian man claimed he was unaware it was an offence to use his cousin's Malaysian identity card to secure employment after he was detained in an enforcement raid at a frozen food processing factory in Pulau Indah here last night. The man, identified only as Restu, was among 36 people detained during Ops Kisar for allegedly using fake MyKad or identity cards belonging to others. He told enforcement officers he had entered Malaysia legally by air but used his cousin's MyKad, whose owner resides in Sabah, to obtain work at the factory.

items.title: Zambry: USM and UKM lead global charge in UN sustainability goals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) and Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) have put Malaysia on the global map after securing places among the world’s top seven universities in the Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Rankings 2026. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said USM was ranked fifth globally and UKM seventh among more than 1,600 universities assessed for their performance in advancing the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). He said USM also retained its top global position in SDG 17: Partnerships for the Goals, highlighting Malaysian universities’ ability to lead international strategic cooperation.

items.title: Bund breach floods 32 homes in Kuala Langat housing area, residents urged to move to safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — A total of 32 homes in Taman Langat Utama 2, Bukit Changgang, Kuala Langat, were affected by flooding after a bund reportedly breached late last night, according to Sinar Harian. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.12am. “Upon arrival, firefighters found that floodwaters had inundated the residential area,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Oil prices fall as tankers exit Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: BEIJING: Oil prices extended their decline on Thursday, edging closer to pre-war levels as stranded tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz following an initial accord to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, easing supply concerns. Prompt-month Brent crude futures for August delivery fell 40 cents, or 0.54 per cent, to US$73.34 a barrel as of 0004 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate fell 27 cents, or 0.38 per cent, to US$70.07 a barrel. Brent had fallen more than US$3 on Wednesday as supply concerns eased, and WTI settled down nearly US$3.

items.title: Is Battersea Power Station overvalued? MOF reveals forensic audit findings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — An independent auditor had submitted two forensic reports regarding allegations about the Battersea Power Station project’s overvaluation of investment assets, concluding that the claims raised are unfounded, and therefore, there is no need for further investigation into Battersea Project Holding Company, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. The ministry said Battersea Project Holding Company had appointed independent forensic auditor Evelyn Partners to review the allegations concerning the project’s financial statements. The MOF added that the Battersea Project Holding Company’s accounts were also audited and certified by PwC, one of the Big Four accounting firms.

items.title: Australia teen social media ban has little impact
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia's social media ban for under 16s has had little impact on teenagers' scrolling habits, researchers said Thursday in one of the first evaluations of the world-leading measures. The researchers surveyed more than 400 young social media users immediately before the restrictions came into effect, and again three months after. "Australia's world-leading social media laws are not failing.

items.title: Ringgit opens firmer on stronger investor sentiment, FDI surge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened firmer against the US dollar today, extending yesterday's gains as investor sentiment improved after data showed Malaysia's foreign direct investment (FDI) surged 41.2 per cent to RM65.9 billion in 2025. The Statistics Department reported yesterday that Malaysia's FDI increased to RM65.9 billion in 2025 from RM46.7 billion in 2024, driven mainly by equity injections from foreign investors and inflows into debt instruments. The department said Malaysia's cumulative FDI position rose to RM1.09 trillion at end-2025, equivalent to 53.7 per cent of gross domestic product, up from 51.4 per cent in 2024, reflecting sustained confidence among foreign investors in the country's economic prospects.

items.title: China's 'future industries' push triggers flood of venture capital, bubble concerns
items.summary: To achieve that goal, Tectronic, established just three months ago, needs to raise 150 million yuan (US$22 million) at a valuation of 1.5 billion yuan, according to its investor presentation. In China, venture capital and private equity investments in the first five months of this year totaled 620 billion yuan (US$91.6 billion), jumping nearly 60 per cent from a year earlier, according to ChinaVenture Investment Consulting. Newly registered venture capital funds in the world's second-largest economy totaled 154 billion yuan during the first five months of 2026, already in excess of last year's total, according to China's fund industry association.

items.title: Malaysia’s fuel subsidy bill triples to RM11b amid West Asia crisis, MOF says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The BUDI MADANI targeted fuel subsidy programme had benefited 14 million Malaysians and involved 11.1 billion litres of fuel as of May 31, 2026, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. MOF said the sales value totalled RM22.1 billion, of which RM11.2 billion was borne by the government as a subsidy. It said that since the advent of the West Asia crisis, BUDI MADANI subsidy spending has increased, on average, by threefold compared with the same period in 2025.

items.title: Stocks edge lower as dollar rises to one-year high
items.summary: NEW YORK: Stocks fell on Wednesday, erasing early Wall Street gains and leaving European shares little changed, as valuation worries continued to weigh on sentiment, while the dollar climbed to a one-year high. Consumer discretionary, industrials and utilities stocks drove gains. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.35 per cent, the S&P 500 fell 0.1 per cent, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.43 per cent.

items.title: ECHR rules against Greece over refugees' rights to family reunification
items.summary: PARIS: The European Court of Human Rights (ECHR) on Wednesday ruled against Greece in three separate cases involving refugees' applications for family reunification, finding that the authorities violated the applicants' rights under the European Convention on Human Rights. The court ordered Greek authorities to pay each applicant €10,000 (over RM47,000) in non-pecuniary damages within three months. According to Greek media reports, the rulings mark the first time the ECHR has found Greece in violation of refugees' family reunification rights in three separate cases.

items.title: Why putting good money after bad is a costly mistake
items.summary: ONE of the oldest and most important maxims in business and investing is: never put good money after bad. The phrase refers to the practice of committing additional resources - money, time or effort - to a failing project or investment in the hope of recovering previous losses. Understanding when to persevere and when to cut losses is essential for successful investing, sound business management, and prudent personal finance.

items.title: Bank Negara steps up ringgit defence, economists say recent weakness is correction [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia will step up efforts to support the ringgit, including attracting greater foreign capital inflows and encouraging government-linked companies and corporates to repatriate earnings held overseas. The move comes as the ringgit weakens by more than four per cent against the US dollar so far in June, prompting the central bank's Financial Markets Committee (FMC) to convene today to assess market conditions. It said the ringgit's recent weakness was due to cautious foreign positioning and rising expectations of higher US interest rates, following its drop to a seven-month low.

items.title: Magnitude 6.9 quake hits northern Japan, no tsunami warning
items.summary: TOKYO: A magnitude 6.9 earthquake struck northern Japan on Thursday, the country's weather agency said, adding that there was no tsunami warning. There were no immediate reports of major damage. The quake occurred off the northern region of Iwate, with the depth of the epicentre at 50 kilometres, the Japan Meteorological Agency said.

items.title: Magnitude 7.1 earthquake strikes Venezuela
items.summary: CARACAS: A magnitude 7.1 earthquake struck Venezuela on Wednesday, the United States Geological Survey (USGS) reported, triggering panic in Caracas, according to AFP journalists. The quake, with an epicentre 21 kilometers (13 miles) east of the coastal town of Moron, occurred at 2204 GMT and was followed by several aftershocks. The tremor had a depth of 10 kilometres and prompted screams of panic at a shopping centre in Caracas, an AFP journalist observed.

items.title: Trump tells Rutte he was 'let down' by Nato countries on Iran
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump told Nato chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was "let down" by members of the transatlantic alliance who did not back his war against Iran. The Nato chief then told journalists after the meeting that Trump is "completely committed to the alliance," and said Washington would "absolutely" protect Europe in case of attack. Trump did not consult Nato ahead of time and key European allies have voiced skepticism over the need for war.

items.title: Traffic flows through Hormuz continue to improve as IMO evacuation begins
items.summary: LONDON: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continued to improve compared to pre-US and Iran agreement levels on Tuesday, with some ships crossing through a new IMO evacuation route, tracking data showed. As of 1500 GMT on Wednesday, maritime tracking firm Kpler recorded 25 transits by commodity ships on Tuesday, and 17 so far on Wednesday. Since June 15, on average 22 ships have crossed every day according to Kpler, compared with less than 10 daily transits between March 1 and June 14.

items.title: Hantavirus outbreak should formally end on July 2: WHO
items.summary: GENEVA: The deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, which sparked international alarm, should be formally declared over on July 2, the World Health Organisation (WHO) announced Wednesday. Virus samples will be worked on to see if tests, treatments and vaccines could be developed for future outbreaks. There were 12 confirmed and one probable case stemming from the MV Hondius, including three deaths, in an outbreak which sparked an international health alert.

items.title: These 21 Johor seats decided two elections — Will they decide a third on July 11?
items.summary: Gambir, once won by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin under the PH banner, reverted to BN. Similarly, Parit Yaani returned to the BN column after unseating Johor PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan. The 21 seats that flipped in 2022: PH to BN Pemanis (PKR to Umno) Kemelah (Amanah to MIC) Tenang (Bersatu to Umno) Gambir (Bersatu to Umno) Serom (Amanah to Umno) Bukit Naning (PKR to Umno) Semerah (PKR to Umno) Yong Peng (DAP to MCA) Parit Yaani (Amanah to Umno) Senggarang (Amanah to Umno) Permas (Bersatu to Umno) Larkin (Bersatu to Umno) Kempas (Bersatu to Umno) Bukit Permai (Bersatu to Umno) Pulai Sebatang (Amanah to Umno) PH to PN Maharani (Amanah to PAS) BN to PN Endau (Umno to Bersatu) PH (Bersatu) to PN (Bersatu) Bukit Kepong (Retained by Sahruddin Jamal, but shifted from PH to PN following Bersatu’s exit) The turnout factor The political shift was inseparable from a collapse in voter participation.

items.title: Nepali recounts Everest escape
items.summary: KATHMANDU: For three days, Nepali mountaineer Dawa Sherpa was trapped deep inside a crevasse on Everest, surviving on biscuits, chocolates and chunks of ice, while back home his family had begun mourning his death. Sherpa had nearly given up hope, until an avalanche partially filled the 8m deep ice crack, creating a route to freedom. Thrall was the last to see Sherpa.

items.title: No deadline for Budi Diesel applications
items.summary: KUCHING: The government has not set a deadline for Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) applications to ensure eligible diesel vehicle owners can benefit from the targeted assistance programme. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said this was to ensure fairness as a person's circumstances could change over time. "A person might not own a diesel vehicle, but they might buy a pickup truck or four-wheel-drive vehicle next month," he said at a Budi Diesel event yesterday.

items.title: After calling it quits, UK's Starmer gets European thanks for warming ties
items.summary: BERLIN: European leaders offered Britain's outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer kind words at a Berlin meeting on Wednesday, days after he announced his resignation following months of pressure and miserable poll numbers. "You have done a great deal to strengthen NATO and foster a united Europe," German Chancellor Friedrich Merz told Starmer at a joint press conference. Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk credited Starmer with patching up relations between the United Kingdom and the European Union after the bruising Brexit process.

items.title: USM ranks 5th on global sustainability list
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) has climbed to fifth place globally in the Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Ratings 2026. 1 global ranking for Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 17: Partnerships for the Goals. The recognition reflects USM's extensive network of partnerships involving government agencies, industry players, non-governmental organisations, communities, higher education institutions and international organisations.

items.title: NPE 2 to support MLFF, says IJM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The New Pantai Expressway Extension (NPE 2) project has been designed to support a future multi-lane free flow (MLFF) tolling system, although rollout remains subject to government policy. IJM Corp Bhd chief executive officer (Toll Division) Chua Lay Hoon said yesterday that MLFF design provisions have been incorporated into the project. Chua said that with completion scheduled for 2029, the four-year construction period gives IJM time to monitor government plans before deciding on toll infrastructure.

items.title: France detects first Ebola case outside Africa in current outbreak
items.summary: FRANCE: France on Wednesday announced the first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor who had flown back from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, which is fighting a major outbreak. It is the first time France has detected Ebola. The health ministry said it had identified "a first positive case of Ebola virus disease on national territory".

items.title: Fuel prices drop: Finance ministry announces lowered petrol and diesel rates amid easing global market
items.summary: Meanwhile, diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will see a 30 sen decrease, bringing the price down to RM4.07 per litre. Other subsidised rates remain at RM2.15 per litre for diesel in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan, RM2.05 per litre under the Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS), and RM2.15 per litre under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS). Retail price Fuel Retail price Changes RON97 RM4.10 per litre (24 Jun 2026 price: RM4.35 per litre) Down 25 sen per litre RON95 RM3.47 per litre (24 Jun 2026 price: RM3.72 per litre) Down 25 sen per litre Diesel (Peninsular Malaysia) RM4.07 per litre (24 Jun 2026 price: RM4.37 per litre) Down 30 sen per litre Current maintained subsidised prices as follows: Fuel Subsidised price RON95 (BUDI95) RM1.99 per litre Diesel (Sabah, Sarawak dan W.P Labuan) RM2.15 per litre Sistem Kawalan Petrol Bersubsidi (SKPS) RM2.05 per litre Sistem Kawalan Diesel Bersubsidi (SKDS) RM2.15 per litre

items.title: Occupational safety department investigating fatal accident at Sungai Buloh water tank cleaning site
items.summary: Hazlina said the investigation was being carried out under Sections 15, 17 and 18 of the Occupational Safety and Health Act 1994, which stipulate the responsibilities of employers, self-employed persons and relevant parties to ensure the safety, health and welfare of workers and others who may be affected by work activities. She reminded employers and workers to comply with occupational safety and health requirements, particularly when carrying out work in confined spaces. “Employers must prioritise the safety and health of all individuals involved in work activities, including vendors and contractors,” she said.

items.title: Education minister files police report over AI-generated video, calling it malicious and defamatory
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) Wanita chief Fadhlina Sidek has filed a police report over the circulation of an AI-generated video spreading false allegations against her. Fadhlina, who also serves as Education Minister, expressed regret in a statement today over the video’s content, which she described as malicious. She also called on all parties to adopt a firm position on issues of slander, character assassination and sexual harassment targeting women politicians.

items.title: Going it alone: Johor BN will not form a joint government with any coalition, says Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) will not form a coalition government with any other political party if it manages to retain power after the state election, said its state chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi. He said the Johor BN’s stance remains firm despite facing criticism since announcing that coalition would contest all 56 state assembly seats for the upcoming state polls on May 16. “This decision was made based on differences in approach and ideology,” he said in his address at the BN Johor state electron candidate announcement ceremony held at the Johor Umno state liaison office here today.

items.title: BN announces Johor election candidate lists, Onn Hafiz spearheading the charge
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) announced its list of 56 candidates for the Johor election today, ending speculation over which candidates the coalition would field. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the list of candidates for the coalition that will contest in the upcoming Johor election. Present were respective component party and affiliate group leaders such as MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong; MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran; People’s Progressive Party (PPP) president Datuk Dr J. Loga Bala Mohan and Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh.

items.title: Not out to get you: New Section 42A meant to rein in illegal racing, dangerous speed trials
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has clarified that the new Section 42A under the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026 is not intended to penalise isolated speeding or ordinary overtaking. Instead, he explained that it targets illegal racing and dangerous speed trials involving competitive conduct. “It targets racing or speed trials involving competitive conduct, challenge based speeding, reckless behaviour, or situations that clearly indicate dangerous activity.

items.title: As traffic violations rise, so must the penalties: Loke defends RM500 fine hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today defended his ministry’s push to raise the penalty for traffic violations to RM500, amid some pushback from Opposition lawmakers who said the move could disproportionately penalise lower income road users. MOT had proposed the increment as part of several amendments to the Road Transport Act tabled Monday, as Loke vowed to impose harsher penalties amid concerns over the rising cases of non-compliance and road safety. Loke explained that the authorities can still impose a lower fine for a violation on a case-to-case basis, countering allegations that the increment would lump lesser serious offences together with more serious violations.

items.title: E-hailing driver fined RM101,750 for importing 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water
items.summary: KLANG, June 24 — An e-hailing driver was fined RM101,750 by the Magistrate’s Court here today after he was found guilty of importing prohibited goods, namely 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water through West Port, Klang, five years ago. He was also ordered to pay customs duty amounting to 20 per cent of the goods’ value, totalling RM2,035, to the Government through the Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) under Section 119A of the Customs Act 1967. The court further ordered all 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water to be forfeited to JKDM under Section 127 of the same Act.

items.title: Malaysia’s rise in competitiveness index reflects civil service efficiency, says Anwar
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, June 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today praised the efficiency of the country’s civil service for contributing to Malaysia’s improved performance in the IMD World Competitiveness Index 2026. No, it is due to the system, the civil service and the entire team, the civil servants. The event was also attended by Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, Deputy Minister of Higher Education Adam Adli Abd Halim, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Public Service director-general Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz, Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah and State Secretary Datuk Azhar Arshad.

items.title: Honda Jazz driver sent to Henry Gurney School for three years over 2025 crash that killed three teens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The Magistrates’ Court in Seremban today ordered the Honda Jazz driver who drove dangerously, causing the deaths of three teenagers in a crash at Matahari Heights last March, to be sent to Henry Gurney School for three years. Magistrate Nurul Azuin Mohd Talhah handed down the sentence after the young offender, now aged 18 years and nine months, pleaded guilty under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, Harian Metro reported. The court also ordered the teenager to be placed under the supervision of a moral officer for one year after completing detention.

items.title: KLIA drug crackdown nets 653kg of cannabis buds, 38 arrests this year
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 24 — Police have seized a total of 653.6 kilogrammes (kg) of cannabis buds at the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) in the first six months of this year. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said this was the result of joint enforcement conducted by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and KLIA security authorities in their ongoing efforts to tighten security measures at the country’s main entry and exit points through screening operations. Meanwhile, commenting on their latest crackdown at KLIA from June 17-22, Shazeli said police conducted three raids and seized cannabis buds weighing 171.6kg worth RM13.73 million and also arrested four individuals, including a Malaysian believed to be a drug mule.

items.title: Man pleads guilty to insulting, threatening posts targeting Terengganu royal family
items.summary: Azman Hakim Hassanuddin, 35, was charged under Section 233(1)(a) of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 with making and initiating the transmission of communications that were grossly offensive and threatening in nature through his X account on Feb 19. Under four of the charges, he was accused of posting communications that were grossly offensive in nature at 2.25 pm, 4.19 pm, 4.20 pm and 4.29 pm, with the intent to annoy others. For the remaining charge, Azman Hakim, who was unrepresented, was accused of posting a threatening communication at 3.46 pm with the intent to threaten another person.

items.title: Anwar: Elections should be between political parties, don’t involve royalty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for elections to be treated strictly as a contest between political parties and not to be linked in any way to the royal institution. He said this when commenting on allegations involving Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, who was said to have made a veiled remark against the Regent of Johor (TMJ) Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim during a recent programme, Sinar Harian reported today. Anwar said he maintained good relations and understanding with TMJ and hoped all parties would avoid escalating tensions over the issue.

items.title: In Sarawak, security guard jailed eight months, fined RM50,000 for offensive Facebook comment against King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — A security guard was sentenced to eight months’ jail and fined RM50,000 by the Sessions Court in Kuching, Sarawak, today after pleading guilty to posting an offensive comment against the His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, via his Facebook account. Judge Iris Awen Jon handed down the sentence on Raden Mohamad Norhakim Raden Ibrahim, 46, and ordered him to serve an additional two months’ jail if he fails to pay the fine, Bernama reported. According to the charge, the accused was charged with making an offensive communication with intent to annoy others through a Facebook post on February 12 last year.

items.title: Malaysia, SE Asia on red alert for severe haze risk in 2026, warns SIIA report
items.summary: SINGAPORE, June 24 — Malaysia, Brunei, Indonesia and Singapore could face severe transboundary haze in the coming months as South-east Asia has been placed on red alert for an elevated haze risk, according to the Singapore Institute of International Affairs (SIIA). In its Haze Outlook 2026 report released on Wednesday, SIIA identified August and September as the peak risk period, with the threat compounded by the El Niño and Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) weather phenomena. “Some meteorologists have also warned of a potential positive IOD, which could further intensify hot and dry weather conditions and increase fire risks across South-east Asia,” it said in a statement issued in conjunction with the report’s release.

items.title: Zara Qairina inquest: Expert says copying handwriting across multiple pages difficult, court hears
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 24 — The Coroner’s Court here today was told that copying another person’s handwriting over many pages would be difficult as differences in writing habits would usually appear. Dr Linthini Gannetion, a forensic document examiner and certified handwriting expert, said that although she would not describe such an act as impossible, it would be difficult because handwriting involves several individual features. Linthini said that before comparing disputed handwriting, an examiner would first identify repeated and unconscious writing habits within a person’s own handwriting using the method ACE-V, namely analyse, compare, evaluate and verify.

items.title: SMK Suria Jaya in Johor to open in September after receiving partial occupation certificate, says MOE
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 24 — Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Suria Jaya here is expected to commence operations in September after the Ministry of Education (MOE) handed over the school project under a Certificate of Partial Occupation (CPO) yesterday. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek expressed appreciation to the project management team, consultants, relevant government agencies, utility providers and contractors for their commitment in ensuring the successful completion of the school project. “God willing, the school will begin operations in September 2026.

items.title: RM1.7b NPE 2 to feature barrier-free MLFF toll system to ease Klang Valley congestion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The New Pantai Expressway Extension Project (NPE 2) will use a barrier-free toll collection system or Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) when fully completed to reduce traffic congestion, especially during peak hours. Chua said the highway extension project would strengthen the integrated highway network in the Klang Valley through better connectivity between the NPE, the Sungai Besi Expressway (BESRAYA) and the proposed Istana-Kiara Expressway (LIKE) in line with the aspirations of the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Master Plan 2040. Commenting on traffic management during the construction work, Chua said a comprehensive Traffic Management Plan (TMP) would be implemented throughout the period to ensure smooth traffic flow and safety of road users.

items.title: SARA programme expanded to include more local products, says finance minister II
items.summary: KUCHING, June 24 — The government continues to strengthen the implementation of the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) programme by opening up opportunities for more products from local entrepreneurs to be marketed based on consumer needs and preferences in a given area. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan stated that this approach not only benefits aid recipients but also helps local entrepreneurs and retailers generate income. “In Kuching, there are local products that are popular among the local community.

items.title: Online scam losses surge to RM2.97b in 2025, says Home Ministry as fraud trend worsens across Malaysia
items.summary: The Home Ministry (KDN) said according to the comparison of various modus operandi, non-existent investments consistently recorded the largest losses with a record of RM848.62 million in 2024, jumping to RM1.46 billion in 2025 and RM361.63 million this year. According to KDN, the trend was closely followed by telecommunications crime which recorded RM497.12 million (2024), RM802.47 million (2025) and RM235.63 million (as of May 2026), while love scams continued to record losses of RM45.87 million (2024), RM47.44 million (2025) and RM17.76 million for the early period of this year. From a geographical aspect, he said Selangor and Kuala Lumpur continued to record the highest losses, with Selangor jumping from RM446.16 million (2024) to RM986.79 million (2025), while Kuala Lumpur increased from RM293.30 million (2024) to RM782.86 million (2025).

items.title: Stats Dept: Domestic tourism spending climbs 15.8pc to RM34b in Q1 2026 amid festive travel boost
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 24 — Domestic tourism expenditure recorded a double-digit growth of 15.8 per cent year-on-year to RM34 billion in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), according to the Bulletin of Malaysia Domestic Tourism Survey - First Quarter 2026 released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today. In a statement, DOSM said Malaysia’s domestic tourism maintained its growth momentum in the first three months of 2026, with total domestic visitors reaching 74.7 million, reflecting a year-on-year increase of 7.2 per cent. The performance was further supported by a 0.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter growth compared to the fourth quarter of 2025, indicating continued stability in domestic travel demand.

items.title: MRCB secures consent judgment in Shah Alam Stadium suit; case against blogger to continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd (MRCB) has secured a consent judgment against one of the defendants in its lawsuit alleging a conspiracy to cause economic damage through online posts about the demolition and redevelopment of the Shah Alam Stadium. According to a statement, the consent judgment was entered at the Shah Alam High Court on June 22 against self-described activist Abdul Razak Ismail, one of two defendants named in the suit. MRCB had sued Abdul Razak and blogger Murray Hunter over a series of posts published on Facebook and Hunter’s Substack platform that questioned the stadium demolition and redevelopment project.
